creator: 000.fbsd@quip.cz
text:
(In reply to Fabian Wenk from comment #8)
You are correct, if port is built on machine with MariaDB installed and running the configure script should find the right path to socket.

security/cyrus-sasl2-sql depends on mysql client or mariadb client library (by default: libmysqlclient.so.20 : databases/mysql57-client) so it should have the right socket if other mysql client tools works / can read it from my.cnf (or equivalent file)

Do you have this in /usr/local/etc/mysql/my.cnf file?
[client-server]
port    = 3306
socket  = /var/run/mysql/mysql.sock

Or does this command show you the right socket path?
# mysql --print-defaults
or
# mariadb --print-defaults
